Story: As a student, I want my profile to show my current belt, stripes, and date of last grading, so I have an at-a-glance view of where I am.
Acceptance notes: Belt options: white/blue/purple/brown/black (coral colours optional); Stripes: 0-4; Last graded date is a single nullable date.
Technical notes: New columns on users table: belt (TEXT), stripes (INTEGER), last_graded_at (TIMESTAMP). Read-only for student; surface on profile header.

Story: As a coach, I want to set and update a student's belt, stripes, and last grading date from their profile.
Acceptance notes: Setting belt or stripes does not auto-set last_graded_at; coach updates that explicitly when a grading happens.
Technical notes: Admin/coach-only mutation endpoint. Audit who set the rank if useful later.

Story: As a student or coach, I want the student profile to default-open on an activity feed showing the top-level items (techniques, camps, matches, profile threads) ordered by their most-recent activity.
Acceptance notes: Each top-level item appears at most once in the feed; multiple activities on the same item collapse into one row showing the latest one (e.g. "Sam watched 'X-guard sweep' 2 hours ago"). Activity that bumps an item to the top includes: comments, replies, video uploads, video watches, status changes, pin events, new threads anchored to it. Rank changes are one-off feed items that don't dedupe. Default tab is Activity; other tabs (Syllabus / Pinned / Camps / My matches) sit alongside.
Technical notes: Per-(viewer, profile_owner) feed query joins activity events to their top-level item and returns one row per item with the latest-activity timestamp + preview. Watch events join via videos.parent_kind/parent_id (see technical notes section 1). See technical notes section 7 for the item taxonomy.

Story: As a student or coach, I want to filter the activity feed by item type (techniques / camps / matches / profile threads / one-off events like rank changes).
Acceptance notes: Chips at the top; multi-select OR semantics like existing tag chips on the syllabus view. "One-off events" filter covers things that don't dedupe (rank changes etc).
Technical notes: Server takes optional item_kind[] filter param; mirrors existing chip pattern on student-techniques page.

Story: As a student or coach, I want to search the activity feed by free text across technique names, camp details, and comment bodies.
Acceptance notes: Plain substring match is fine for v1; no fuzzy/typo tolerance needed.
Technical notes: SQLite FTS5 over a denormalised search column, or simple LIKE if volume is small. Decide based on size.

Story: As a student or coach, I want to start a thread on a student's profile to ask a question or kick off a conversation, not anchored to any video.
Acceptance notes: Body supports text, @-mentions, video reply attachments.
Technical notes: New thread entity. parent_kind=profile.

Story: As a student or coach, I want to reply to any thread inline.
Acceptance notes: Reply visibility inherits parent thread's visibility scope.
Technical notes: Comment table FK parent_thread_id and optional parent_comment_id.

Story: As a coach, I want to record a fresh video reply within a thread, OR upload a video I've already recorded.
Acceptance notes: Both flows land in the same reply slot in the thread. Recording uses the device camera; upload picks a file or comes from the device gallery.
Technical notes: Reply video uses the standard upload pipeline. Mark video as scope=thread to skip global promotion prompts.

Story: As a student with Footage Submitter permission, I want to record or upload a video reply within a thread.
Acceptance notes: Same constraints as CX-008. Without Footage Submitter, student can only reply with text.
Technical notes: Gated by new Permission::SubmitFootage.

Story: As a coach or student, I want video replies to support text comments within the thread but not be able to spawn new top-level threads of their own.
Acceptance notes: Avoids endless reply chains. Video replies still support comments inside the parent thread.
Technical notes: Enforce at API: only original thread anchors accept new thread creation.

Story: As a coach or student, I want to @-mention a technique in a comment and have it inline as a tappable card.
Acceptance notes: Mention syntax in body; renders as card on display with name + description preview.
Technical notes: Store mentions as structured tokens in body, e.g. {"type":"technique","id":42}, alongside plain text.

Story: As a coach or student, I want to @-mention a specific video (optionally at a timestamp) and have it inline as a playable card.
Acceptance notes: Picker is categorized so the author can find the video by where it lives: techniques in the library (with their attached videos), raw uploaded videos (loose / profile / camp), camp videos, match videos. Searchable by name/title.
Technical notes: Same structured-token storage. Video mention may include a timestamp_seconds field. Picker pulls from a unified video index across all parent kinds.

Story: As a coach, when I @-mention a video that's hidden from the recipient student (globally, or for them in their syllabus), I want the app to prompt me with the right unhide action before publishing.
Acceptance notes: Prompt copy varies by hide source. Globally hidden: "This student can't see this video because it is hidden globally. Unhide globally?" (affects every student). Hidden for them in their syllabus: "This student can't see this video because it is hidden for them. Unhide for them?" (only this student). Confirm performs the corresponding unhide; Cancel publishes the @-mention without acting (recipient sees a no-access card per CX-014).
Technical notes: Pre-publish check: query videos.hidden_at first, then the syllabus-scoped per-student visibility (CX-019). Branch the prompt on which is the blocker. If both apply, surface the global option first since it's the bigger lever.

Story: As a student, when I @-mention a video I can't personally see, the mention is published without granting me visibility.
Acceptance notes: Asymmetric by design: students don't auto-grant themselves access. May rarely trigger in practice, but included for exhaustive coverage.
Technical notes: Skip the visibility-grant prompt for student authors.

Story: As any coach, I want to view the activity feed of any student in the gym.
Acceptance notes: No per-coach gating in v1; substitute teaching makes per-coach scoping noisy.
Technical notes: Coach role has read-all on student activity. Revisit if we add multi-tenancy.

Story: As a coach or student, I want to add emoji reactions (e.g. 👍 ✅ 🔥) to a comment, thread, or video reply.
Acceptance notes: Multiple emoji choices; reactions visible to everyone who can see the parent.
Technical notes: New reactions table. Defer until v1 ships and we see if friction is real.

Story: As a student, I want to opt out of letting coaches see my video watch activity.
Acceptance notes: Default is opt-in (visible to coaches). Toggle on profile.
Technical notes: Add opt_out flag on users. Filter watch event aggregates in coach-facing queries when set.

Story: As a coach, I want to upload a video unattached to anything (loose) and later attach it to a camp / technique / match.
Acceptance notes: Loose videos surface as suggestions in the categorized picker (CX-012) and in promotion flows. Coach can filter/search by what they remember (date uploaded, who uploaded, body of any associated thread).
Technical notes: video.parent_kind nullable; promotion endpoint sets parent_kind + parent_id later. Loose video index needs metadata good enough for context-driven search.

Story: As a coach, I want per-student video visibility overrides to be context-scoped (the syllabus has its own override layer, each camp has its own override layer), so that hiding a video for a student in one context doesn't ripple into other contexts.
Acceptance notes: Library browse: only global hide applies; no per-student overrides. Syllabus view: global hide + the syllabus-scoped per-student override layer. Each camp view: global hide + that camp's per-student override layer (see CC-015). The same student can have a video hidden in their syllabus but visible inside a camp, and vice versa.
Technical notes: Refactor: today's video_student_visibility becomes the syllabus-scoped layer (rename + constrain query usage to syllabus context). camp_video_visibility (CC-015) is the camp-scoped layer. Library / thread / pinned contexts query only videos.hidden_at.

Story: As a student or coach, I want the syllabus to live in its own dedicated tab on the student profile (with its own status / attempts / notes), not be the default mixed view.
Acceptance notes: Syllabus becomes one tab among Activity / Syllabus / Pinned / Camps. The current default-landing-on-technique-list behaviour moves to the Syllabus tab.
Technical notes: Move existing student-techniques page to a tab. Activity feed becomes the default. Existing deep-link routes (e.g. /student/<id>?focus=) still work, just land on the Syllabus tab.

Story: As a coach or student, I want syllabus attempts to support inline threads (with @-mentions and video replies) instead of just free-text notes.
Acceptance notes: Phase-2 unification: any attempt has a thread underneath it like any other anchorable item.
Technical notes: Replace attempt.note field with a thread attached to the attempt. Migration of existing notes into a seed comment.

Story: As a coach or student, I want a syllabus-scoped thread to exist on the technique itself (not just on individual attempts), so we can have a running conversation about the technique within the syllabus context.
Acceptance notes: The technique-level thread is visible in the syllabus view by default. When the student views the same technique from the global library or their pinned list, the syllabus thread surfaces alongside it under the syllabus-context section, governed by the same toggle as SD-008/SD-009.
Technical notes: Thread.parent_kind=syllabus_technique anchored to (student_id, technique_id). Same syllabus-context surfacing logic that drives SD-008.

Story: As a coach or admin, I want to delete any student comment or thread, so I can moderate inappropriate content, fix mistakes, or remove abuse.
Acceptance notes: Soft delete by default: the comment/thread is replaced by a "deleted by <coach/admin>" placeholder so replies still make sense in context. The original author is notified. Hard delete (full removal) available to admin only.
Technical notes: Add deleted_at + deleted_by_id columns on threads and comments. Cascade behaviour on replies: keep them with a stub parent reference, don't cascade-delete.

Story: As a coach or admin, I want to delete any student-uploaded video, so I can manage storage costs and moderate inappropriate content.
Acceptance notes: Soft delete via existing videos.deleted_at mechanism. Watch history preserved. Threads attached to the video timestamp survive but the video card shows "deleted". Hard delete + storage reclaim is a separate admin flow if needed.
Technical notes: Existing videos.deleted_at column already supports this. Add coach moderation endpoint that flips it and records who/when.

Story: As a student or coach, I want in-app notifications when someone replies to my thread/comment or @-mentions me, so I don't miss conversations.
Acceptance notes: Count badge on the navbar. Opening the notification center lists unread items most-recent-first with direct links to source. Per-item mark-as-read and a "mark all read" action.
Technical notes: New notifications table: (id, user_id, kind, source_id, source_kind, created_at, read_at). Server generates rows on reply / @-mention events. Per-user read state.

Story: As a student or coach, I want in-app notifications when someone reacts to my comment, thread, or video reply.
Acceptance notes: Same notification center surface as CX-025. Reactions trigger lightweight notifications (probably grouped: "3 people reacted to your comment" rather than one per reaction).
Technical notes: Extends notifications table with reaction kind. Bundle near-simultaneous reactions into a single notification.

Story: As a coach or student, I want a visual indicator in the activity feed showing where unseen activity ends, so I can quickly tell what's new since I last looked at this profile.
Acceptance notes: Items above the indicator have new activity since I last viewed this profile; items below are unchanged since my last visit. Renders as a thin divider with text like "Caught up below this line". The indicator updates as I scroll past items (interacting with or scrolling past an item marks it seen).
Technical notes: Per-(viewer, item) seen_at tracking, same shape as today's student_technique_views. Update on scroll-past or interaction. The feed query returns items with their seen status so the indicator placement is server-driven.

Story: As a coach, I want to create a generic camp for a student, with a name and optional description.
Acceptance notes: No date or event required.
Technical notes: New camps table: id, student_id, coach_id, name, description, created_at, archived_at, competition_id (nullable).

Story: As a coach, I want to create a competition camp by attaching competition metadata (event name and date) to a camp.
Acceptance notes: Competition is a separate entity (CC-004) the camp links to.
Technical notes: Camp.competition_id FK to new competitions table.

Story: As a coach, I want to promote an existing generic camp into a competition camp by linking it to a competition.
Acceptance notes: One-click action from the camp page; choose existing competition or create one on the spot.
Technical notes: PUT /api/camps/<id> with competition_id.

Story: As a coach, I want to create a competition entity (name, date) that students in the gym can register for.
Acceptance notes: Competitions are gym-wide. Coach creates; students opt in.
Technical notes: New competitions table: id, name, date, created_by_id, created_at.

Story: As a student, I want to register myself for an upcoming competition so my coach can build a camp for me without chasing.
Acceptance notes: Registering opens a (student, competition) record. Coach is notified via the dashboard or activity feed.
Technical notes: competition_registrations table: id, student_id, competition_id, registered_at, registered_by_id.

Story: As a coach, I want to register a student for a competition on their behalf (covers the unaware / lazy / forgetful case).
Acceptance notes: Coach can also un-register.
Technical notes: Same table as CC-005; registered_by_id captures the actor.

Story: As a coach, I want a per-competition page that lists every student registered for it, with quick links into each student's camp for that comp.
Acceptance notes: Sits at /competitions/<id>. Roster + camp progress summary.
Technical notes: GET /api/competitions/<id>/students.

Story: As a coach, I want to add techniques to a camp by picking from the global library using the same autocomplete UX as the syllabus.
Acceptance notes: Picker shows existing library techniques; clear visual indication on scoped techniques surfaced from CC-012/CC-014.
Technical notes: New camp_techniques table: (camp_id, technique_id, position). Reuses existing technique picker component.

Story: As a coach, I want to create a new technique inside a camp that gets added to the global library at the same time, so other students can also benefit.
Acceptance notes: One of the two required choices at create time. The other is CC-010.
Technical notes: Create technique with scope=global + camp_technique row.

Story: As a coach, I want to create a new technique inside a camp that stays scoped to this camp/student only, not added globally.
Acceptance notes: The other required choice (vs CC-009). Coach must pick at creation time; no silent default.
Technical notes: Create technique with scope=scoped, scoped_camp_id set. Doesn't surface in the global library list, only in the dedicated scoped view (CC-012).

Story: As a coach, I want to promote a scoped technique into the global library, and pick individually which existing content (specific videos, notes) comes along vs stays scoped.
Acceptance notes: Granular picker, not all-or-nothing. Each video and note on the scoped technique has a "bring along" checkbox in the promote dialog.
Technical notes: PUT promote-to-global with payload listing which child content IDs to migrate.

Story: As a coach, I want a dedicated "scoped techniques" view (not mingled with the global library) that lists every scoped technique across all my students, badged with the student/camp they're private to.
Acceptance notes: Sits as a sibling to the global library view. Coach uses it to audit, find candidates to promote, or find scoped techniques to copy across students.
Technical notes: New /scoped-techniques page; GET /api/techniques?scope=scoped.

Story: As a coach in the scoped-techniques view, I want to filter and sort by student, by camp, by recency, and by content size (how many videos/notes are attached).
Acceptance notes: Helps surface promotion candidates and stale scoped techniques.
Technical notes: Query params on the scoped-techniques endpoint.

Story: As a coach, when I'm adding a technique to a student's camp and a similar scoped technique exists for another student, I want the picker to surface it as a suggestion with one-step options to (a) copy it to this student with chosen content or (b) promote it globally with chosen content.
Acceptance notes: Surfaces at the moment of friction (add-to-camp picker), not buried in admin.
Technical notes: Picker shows matching scoped techniques as suggestions with action menu. Uses the same granular content-picker as CC-011.

Story: As a coach, I want to hide specific library videos within a camp, without affecting the student's view of that video in other contexts (other camps, syllabus, library browse).
Acceptance notes: E.g. "for this comp, don't watch that no-gi variation". Hide is camp-scoped only. The camp already belongs to one student, so the override is naturally per-student.
Technical notes: camp_video_visibility table: (camp_id, video_id, visible BOOLEAN, set_by_id, set_at). Layered on top of videos.hidden_at; orthogonal to syllabus-scoped overrides (CX-019).

Story: As a coach or footage-submitting student, I want to upload a raw video directly to a competition camp (not tied to any match yet).
Acceptance notes: Use case: have comp footage but haven't broken it into matches yet, or matches are too messy to assign.
Technical notes: Video.parent_kind=camp, parent_id=camp_id.

Story: As a coach or footage-submitting student, I want to upload a raw video directly to a student's profile, not tied to any camp at all.
Acceptance notes: Use case: historical footage, or a competition the coach didn't know about.
Technical notes: Video.parent_kind=profile, parent_id=student_id.

Story: As a coach, I want to upload a video to a specific camp technique, choosing at upload time whether it lives only in this camp or is promoted to the global technique.
Acceptance notes: If the parent technique is scoped (not global), the promote choice is hidden; there's nothing global to promote to.
Technical notes: Upload flow includes scope-choice radio when parent technique is global.

Story: As a coach, I want to attach a previously-uploaded loose video to a camp, technique, or match after the fact.
Acceptance notes: Promotion workflow for video lifecycle. Reverse of CX-018. Picker uses the unified categorized picker (CX-012) so the coach can find the video by context they remember.
Technical notes: PUT /api/videos/<id>/attach with new parent_kind + parent_id.

Story: As a coach or student, I want to log each match in a competition camp: result (W/L/D) and how (submission with detail, points, decision).
Acceptance notes: Students more likely to do this than coaches; this is meaningful self-recording for the student. Submission detail is free-text (e.g. "kimura from north-south"). Points/decision capture is minimal.
Technical notes: matches table: id, registration_id, result (enum), method (enum), method_detail (text), occurred_at, created_by_id. Both coach and student have permission to create/edit matches on their own registration.

Story: As a coach or footage-submitting student, I want to upload raw match video against a specific match, so it's clearly associated with that result.
Acceptance notes: Multiple videos per match allowed.
Technical notes: Video.parent_kind=match, parent_id=match_id.

Story: As a coach, after a competition I want to attach existing camp techniques to specific matches as analysis (e.g. "this match -> drill kimura escape next month").
Acceptance notes: Many-to-many: a technique can be linked to multiple matches and vice versa.
Technical notes: match_techniques table: (match_id, camp_technique_id).

Story: As a coach watching any competition or match video, I want to start a thread at a specific timestamp on the video to give the student feedback.
Acceptance notes: Same primitive as library video threads.
Technical notes: Thread.parent_kind=video_timestamp, with anchor seconds.

Story: As a student, I want to reply to my coach's video thread feedback inline.
Acceptance notes: Reply lives in the same thread; inherits visibility (student + coaches).
Technical notes: Standard reply mechanism (CX-007).

Story: As a coach, I want to grant a student the Footage Submitter permission so they can upload videos to their own profile and to camps they're part of.
Acceptance notes: Promotable per-student permission. Reason it's gated: video storage costs money, organisation matters, and an open free upload surface invites abuse. Distinct from being a registered competitor; non-competing students can also be Footage Submitters.
Technical notes: New Permission::SubmitFootage. Per-user flag, granted by coach.

Story: As a coach, I want to revoke a student's Footage Submitter permission.
Acceptance notes: Simple toggle. Revocation does not delete past uploads. Coach may revoke if abuse is observed or storage costs become an issue.
Technical notes: Permission revoke endpoint.

Story: As a coach, I want a student's profile to show their camp history: count of camps done, recent match results summary, list of past and current camps with quick links in.
Acceptance notes: Avoid imputing "most-drilled" or other vague aggregates. Keep it concrete: counts, results, links.
Technical notes: Aggregate query across camps + matches for this student.

Story: As a student, I want to see my own camp history on my profile.
Acceptance notes: Same data as CC-027; student view of their own camps.
Technical notes: Same query; permission gate on student-views-self.

Story: As a coach, I want to mark a camp as completed/archived once we're done with it, so it falls out of active views but stays referenceable.
Acceptance notes: Archived camps still appear in cross-camp history, in the activity feed, and can be referenced when starting a new camp (CC-035). Do not appear in active rosters.
Technical notes: camp.archived_at timestamp.

Story: As a coach, I want to start a new camp that explicitly references a previous camp as its origin, instead of trying to re-open an archived one.
Acceptance notes: E.g. "Sam's IBJJF 2026 prep, building from Sam's Naga 2025 camp". Replaces "unarchive". Avoids one camp meaning two distinct prep cycles.
Technical notes: camp.references_camp_id FK (nullable). New camp's UI shows "builds on <previous camp>" with link.

Story: As a student, I want a dedicated "my matches" view on my profile that aggregates all my match footage across every camp (current and archived) in one place, so I can revisit a clip without remembering which camp it belonged to.
Acceptance notes: Reverse-chronological list of matches with result, method, camp it belonged to, and direct links into playback and any existing threads on the footage. Sits as a tab on the profile alongside Activity / Syllabus / Pinned / Camps. The discussion / suggestion / new-camp-flow value built on top is captured by CC-032 / CC-033 / CC-035 / CC-036.
Technical notes: Aggregate query across matches FK'd to this student via competition_registrations. Reuse existing video player and thread surfacing.

Story: As a student watching my historical match footage, I want to start a discussion thread at a specific moment to flag it for coach attention.
Acceptance notes: Thread anchors to the video timestamp like CC-023, but initiated by the student. Goes into the activity feed for student + coaches.
Technical notes: Same Thread.parent_kind=video_timestamp mechanism; student permission to create threads on their own footage.

Story: As a student watching my own footage (current or historical), I want to suggest a technique from the global library that I think I should work on, attached to the moment in the video.
Acceptance notes: Lets the student carry the curation burden where they're best placed to (they're the one watching, they know what felt wrong). Suggestion is visible to the coach in their queue.
Technical notes: New technique_suggestions table: (student_id, technique_id, anchor_video_id, anchor_seconds, status enum, created_at). Status: pending / approved / replaced / dismissed.

Story: As a coach, I want to review a student's technique suggestion and either approve it (adds the technique to a relevant camp), replace it with a different technique I think fits better, or dismiss it.
Acceptance notes: Suggestion queue surfaces on the coach dashboard alongside reset requests and pending approvals. Acting on a suggestion records why (approval vs replacement vs dismiss).
Technical notes: Update technique_suggestion + add to camp_technique if approved.

Story: As a coach starting a new camp for a student, I want to reference content from a previous camp (matches, threads, scoped techniques) directly, so the new camp builds on insight from the prior one.
Acceptance notes: Picker on new-camp creation: "seed from previous camp?". Coach picks which historical matches/threads/techniques to link to (not copy: link).
Technical notes: Link table between new camp and referenced items. Matches and threads stay attached to their original camp; new camp shows them as referenced.

Story: As a coach, I want raw match footage from a previous camp to be referenceable as first-class content in a new camp (e.g. attach as analysis to a new camp technique).
Acceptance notes: Specifically: "look at this clip from your last comp" attached to a current camp's technique. Video itself stays in its original parent (the prior match); the new camp just links to it.
Technical notes: camp_technique_referenced_videos table: (camp_technique_id, video_id). Video visibility is checked against the student in the new context.

Story: As a coach, I want to promote a student's pinned (self-directed) technique into one of their camps, carrying the conversation along.
Acceptance notes: The pinned technique becomes a camp_technique in the chosen camp. Notes are already shared by (student, technique) per SD-004, so they appear in the camp view automatically. Threads and comments tied to the pinned context get linked into the camp context so coach feedback stays accessible. Pinned relationship can stay (technique is both pinned and in a camp) or auto-unpin, coach's choice.
Technical notes: Promotion endpoint creates camp_technique. Notes auto-shared via (student, technique) key. Thread/comment linking is its own design question; default is link rather than copy.

Story: As a student, I want to browse the full global technique library, not just techniques assigned to me.
Acceptance notes: Browse view shows all global library techniques with name, description, tags, video count. Scoped techniques (CC-010) belonging to other students are not shown.
Technical notes: Add Permission::BrowseLibrary to student role. New /library page for students.

Story: As a student, I want to watch any video on a technique from the global library that hasn't been globally hidden by a coach.
Acceptance notes: Library context ignores syllabus-scoped per-student overrides (those only apply within the syllabus view). Camp-scoped overrides don't apply here either.
Technical notes: Visibility check: videos.hidden_at IS NULL only. See CX-019.

Story: As a student, I want to pin a technique from the global library to my personal "working on" list.
Acceptance notes: Pinning creates an activity feed event for me and is visible to my coaches as an initiative signal. Pinned techniques appear on a dedicated Pinned tab on my profile.
Technical notes: New table: pinned_techniques (student_id, technique_id, pinned_at). Pinned tab on profile shows the list.

Story: As a student, I want to leave personal notes on a technique I've pinned.
Acceptance notes: Notes are private to me + my coaches. The same notes appear across every view of the same (student, technique) pair: syllabus, pinned, camp. There is one shared notes record per (student, technique); editing it anywhere updates it everywhere. Editing moves the technique to the top of my activity feed.
Technical notes: Notes live on student_techniques and are keyed by (student, technique). Pinning is a separate relationship that points at the same row (or creates one if none exists). See technical exploration doc for the pinned-notes model.

Story: As a student, I want to unpin a technique from my personal list when I'm not actively working on it anymore.
Acceptance notes: Unpinning removes it from the Pinned tab. Unpinning is NOT an activity feed event (no fanfare for stopping work).
Technical notes: Set pinned_techniques.unpinned_at or delete the row. Notes/comments persist; can be re-surfaced by re-pinning.

Story: As a student viewing a pinned technique that's also in my syllabus (current or past), I want to see the syllabus context inline: status, attempts, notes from when it was in my syllabus.
Acceptance notes: Surfaces alongside or above the pinned-context content, clearly separated visually.
Technical notes: Query for existing student_technique row(s) for this (student + technique) pair from the syllabus tab.

Story: As a student, I want a toggle to hide the syllabus context when it's noisy or no longer relevant.
Acceptance notes: Toggle is per-technique or per-view; remember choice in URL state.
Technical notes: UI toggle; preference saved in URL like existing filter chips.

Story: As a student, I want to leave a private timestamped comment on a library video or technique (whether or not I've pinned it).
Acceptance notes: Leaving a comment is an activity feed event (visible to me + coaches). If the technique isn't already pinned, leaving the first comment does NOT auto-pin; pinning stays an explicit user action.
Technical notes: Comment with visibility=private_to_owner_and_coaches. Activity feed item created on comment.

Story: As a coach, I want to reply to a student's private comment on a library video or technique.
Acceptance notes: Reply visibility matches parent (student + coaches).
Technical notes: Standard reply mechanism.

Story: As a coach, I want to leave a broadcast comment on a library video that every student can see.
Acceptance notes: Use case: "everyone notice the grip switch at 0:30" or after-the-fact context where I don't want to record a whole new video.
Technical notes: Comment with visibility=broadcast.

Story: As a coach, I want my dashboard's Initiative roster signal to include self-directed activity: pins, library video watches, library comments, technique suggestions.
Acceptance notes: Two open questions, to revisit in design: (a) should we expand the Initiative tab semantics or split into a richer "initiative breakdown" view? (b) what's the right time window?
Technical notes: Update Initiative tab query to union new signal sources. May want a new initiative_signals aggregation table if queries get expensive.

Story: As a coach, I want to see on a student's profile what techniques they've been studying on their own recently, so I can ask about it in class.
Acceptance notes: Recent pins + recent library watches + recent comments + recent suggestions surface on the activity feed and on a profile header summary.
Technical notes: Activity feed item kinds (CX-003) cover this naturally; profile header summary is a small aggregation.

Story: As a graduated student, I want to retain access to my completed syllabus as its own read-only view, so I can refer back to the coach's notes and videos on those techniques.
Acceptance notes: Syllabus tab continues to exist after graduation in read-only mode (no new attempts, no status changes). Techniques I pin from the library still work normally; syllabus context (SD-008) still surfaces.
Technical notes: Existing graduated_at flag stays; Syllabus tab respects it. No data deletion.

Story: As a student, I want my profile to show my attendance heatmap (year view), same chart shape as the attempts heatmap, so I can see my training streak.
Acceptance notes: Heatmap aggregates attendance events by date.
Technical notes: Depends on attendance data source. Use existing AttemptHeatmap component shape.

Story: As a coach, I want to see each student's attendance heatmap on their profile.
Acceptance notes: Same chart as OS-001; surfaces in coach view.
Technical notes: Coach permission to view attendance.

Story: As a coach, I want to set and update a student's belt, stripes, and last grading date from their profile.
Acceptance notes: Same as CX-002 (this is the OSM-themed lens of it); do not implement separately.
Technical notes: Folds into CX-002.

Story: As a coach, after a class I want to mark which students I personally worked with, so I have a record of where my time goes.
Acceptance notes: Marking is per-class if class roster is available; otherwise per-date. Low-friction is the goal: ideally one tap per student from a pre-populated class roster.
Technical notes: attention_events table: (coach_id, student_id, class_id nullable, marked_at).

Story: As a coach, my "I worked with X" log is private to me; other coaches see their own logs, not mine.
Acceptance notes: Per-coach scoping. No cross-coach visibility.
Technical notes: Filter attention_events by coach_id in coach views.

Story: As a coach, I want to see on a student's profile how recently I've personally worked with them, so I can balance my attention across students.
Acceptance notes: Surfaces as a recency field ("last worked with: 3 days ago") on the student row in my roster, and on the student profile header.
Technical notes: Query attention_events scoped to current coach + student.

Story: As a coach, I want the Initiative / Recent / Quiet roster tabs to factor in attendance and my own attention-recency, not just syllabus updates.
Acceptance notes: Quiet = no attention from me + no student-initiative in last N days. Initiative includes self-directed activity (SD-013). SD-013's part can ship v1; the attendance/attention parts wait on OS-004/006 in phase-2.
Technical notes: Update queries powering the roster tabs.

Story: As a coach, I want a "grading day" entity where I assemble a list of students to assess on a specific date. Sillybus should suggest candidates by surfacing context (rank, time since last grading, recent attendance, recent initiative, recent attention) so I curate by elimination rather than from scratch.
Acceptance notes: Coach makes the final call; the app's job is to surface every relevant signal to inform that call, not to make recommendations directly. Suggestion view is essentially the existing roster filtered + sorted by "ready signals".
Technical notes: New grading_day + grading_day_students tables. Suggestion view is a query joining ranks + attendance + initiative + attention.

Story: As a coach, I want Sillybus to connect to our gym's student check-in software so attendance data flows in automatically.
Acceptance notes: Pending choice of platform + API access. May only give per-class roster, may give more.
Technical notes: External API integration; store only foreign user ID + non-PII attendance data.

Story: As a coach, after a class ends I want to see a list of students who checked into that class with one-click "I worked with this student" buttons, to minimise friction on attention marking.
Acceptance notes: Highest-value flow for attention-marking adoption. Hard-blocked on OS-009.
Technical notes: Requires class roster from check-in API.

Story: As a coach, I want Sillybus to know which classes I personally teach so my attention-marking flow defaults to my classes, AND so the activity feeds and rosters I see can be scoped to students who train in classes I cover.
Acceptance notes: Hard problem: substitutes, students attending multiple classes per week with multiple coaches, no clean coach-to-student ownership. Has implications across the app for which coach sees which student's activity. Firmly in "later" bucket; needs further exploration before any concrete design.
Technical notes: Requires class schedule + coach assignment data from check-in API. Likely needs an ownership/affinity model on top.
